title: Our Favorite Budget TV Is on Sale
author: Brad Bourque
fullText: This 55-inch Vizio isn’t the fanciest, but you could do a lot worse for under $300.Looking for a new television, but don't want to break the bank on fancy QD-OLED technology and features you may not need? This 55-inch screen from Vizio is marked down to $237 at Walmart, a 10 percent discount from its usual, already quite low price. It would make a great second TV for your garage or guest room.With a plain old LED panel, the Vizio isn't going to turn any heads or impress your most keen-eyed friends, but it's more than good enough for a night in catching up on your stories. It still has full support for Dolby Vision, plus HDR10+ for all your high dynamic range needs. It even has some gaming chops, with 60 Hz at 4K, or 120 Hz at 1080p with motion enhancement turned on, good enough for all but the newest consoles and gaming computers. If your device supports it, the Vizio has variable refresh rate (VRR) to match the screen's current refresh rate to the frame rate of your game to reduce tearing.While it has its own built-in smart interface from Vizio, it also supports a variety of wireless connections, including both Apple AirPlay 2 and Google Cast, so both Android and iOS users can take advantage. If you want to include it in your smart home automations, it also hooks up to Apple Home, Google Assistant, and Amazon Alexa. If you use the mobile app, you can even leverage voice controls to easily find your favorite show or movie, and Wi-Fi 6 means you have one of the latest connection types for faster connections and less buffering. For ports, you'll get access to three HDMI 2.1 ports, although unfortunately none of them feature eARC, which is a reasonable compromise given the extremely low price point.If you're ready to pull the trigger on this discounted budget screen, make sure to head over to Walmart and grab the 55-inch Vizio for just $237. If you're curious what spending a little bit more would get you, we have a whole guide with our favorite televisions that includes awesome QLED and OLED options with advanced features.Brad Bourque is a contributor for WIRED with a focus on computing hardware and peripherals. He’s a lifelong Oregonian and an avid Portland Thorns and Timbers supporter. On his days off, you can find him riding his ebike around Portland or taking his dog to the park. ... summary: This 55-inch Vizio isn’t the fanciest, but you could do a lot worse for under $300.
